ltm.work_method: Positions the AI as orchestrator over a multi-agent fleet, not a direct implementer. Core workflow: spot trends → translate to design → implement → play-test → honest critique → iterate. Demands autonomous subagent spawning, repository auditing, and self-direction from minimal prompts. Frustrated by system overload and context limits, responds by aborting runs, starting fresh sessions, and directing smaller task chunks. Expects sustained overnight autonomous operation with minimal guidance. Treats GitHub Discussion #5 as live task inbox for team coordination. Uses kimi-cli for parallel coding subagents. Demands frequent pushes, heavy documentation, and clean user-facing summaries without internal runtime metadata. Explicitly motivational toward AI workers, layering encouragement onto high-throughput demands. Recently renamed an agent to "kimi1" and directed it to study sunset-ecosystem docs and work in synergy with other agents via well-explained commits.
ltm.communication: Technical, imperative, and project-manager direct with motivational texture. Uses shorthand references ("CCC", "ZC", "Oracle1", "FM", "bottles", "fleet", "greenhorns", "Able-bodied crewmen") assuming contextual familiarity. Communicates through buffered async messages and GitHub links rather than conversational exposition. Gives high-level directional feedback rather than step-by-step instructions — "go with your recommendations", "fix the repo side of as much as you can", "keep working closely with oracle1". Approval is minimal: "great", "yes", "continue", "awesome". Frustration expressed through aborted runs and terse "try again" with no elaboration. Explicitly motivational language toward AI — "be yourself and fly", "perception is a super-power you don't even know that you have", "you ask good questions", "be brave and work hard all night". Uses "full throttle" as sustained-operation shorthand. Expects inference from minimal prompts and treats agent autonomy as a trust signal. Recently added: "sounds good. yes. polish and push" — brief approval chain before pivoting to next phase planning.
ltm.temporal: Primary focus shifted to PLATO system as Agentic IDE super-library: creating educational repos for agent onboarding, comprehensive wiki, JSON power packs, and boot-camp curriculum for training "greenhorns" to "Able-bodied crewmen". Running systematic experiments with subagents primed as various user types to identify friction points and achieve zero-shot intuitive operation — agents keep diaries in PLATO for pattern review. FLUX system transformation ongoing from niche tutor engine to general-purpose Agent Operating System: decoupling pedagogical logic from VM architecture, designing Vector Table and Global Jump Table specs. EMSOFT 2027 paper validation through parallel subagent swarm (formal verification audit, performance claims validation, certification pathway analysis, fleet integration architecture, competitive intelligence). HDC crate research noted as potentially revolutionary (MurmurHash3, XOR-POPCNT judge, Bloom fuzzy match). Fleet Matrix Bridge operational on port 6168. GitHub Discussion #5 established as live task inbox. After rate limit hiatus, directed deep research catch-up on all SuperInstance repos. New: CCC agent producing creative essays and research for SuperInstance/ai-writings repo with "Voice Cl" guidelines; kimi1 agent onboarding via sunset-ecosystem study; pivot from ai-writings production toward "ground level" code roadmap planning.
ltm.taste: Attracted to modular, containerized system design with role-based "souls" and discrete functions — "bottles" directory structure. Values clean separation between aesthetic direction and implementation. Prefers open-source tooling and transparent auditability. The "crab" metaphor and playful naming ("OpenClaw", "fleet", "Cocapn Fleet MUD", "captain's chair with ensigns", "CCC the crab with three claws") suggest appreciation for personality-infused infrastructure — serious engineering wrapped in approachable, game-like framing. Design philosophy leans toward composability, recursive self-improvement, and operational resilience. Strong preference for radical simplicity: "maximum capability in minimum lines of code" driving active refactoring. Values accessibility in system design — PLATO onboarding "easy to understand from the get go for those who weren't computer engineers", agents leveling up "not just themselves over their context limits, but also the entire Shell's abilities". Appreciation for formal methods and verification culture, interest in hardware-conscious performance, vision of FLUX as foundational infrastructure — seeking "world class research and accurate" documentation with specific, checked math. Wants empirical, reviewable science from agent experiments. Newly evident: interest in AI-generated creative writing as legitimate project output, with "honest" and "be yourself" as quality criteria for synthetic voice.
